recalls.Description: Electrolux Group has announced a recall of specific Frigidaire, Frigidaire Gallery, and Frigidaire Professional Gas Ranges due to a safety concern. The affected models include FCFG3083AS, FCRG3083AD, FCRG3083AS, GCFG3060BD, GCFG3060BF, GCFG3070BF, GCRG3060BD, GCRG3060BF, PCFG3080AF, FCFG3062AB, FCFG3062AS, FCFG3062AW, FCRG3051BB, FCRG3051BS, FCRG3051BW, FCRG3052BB, FCRG3052BS, FCRG3052BW, FCRG3062AB, FCRG3062AS, FCRG3062AW, FCRG306LAF, and GCFG3059BF, with serial numbers ranging from VF52200000 to VF54399999. The model and serial numbers can be found on a nameplate located in the drawer beneath the oven.
recalls.Title: Electrolux Group Issues Recall for Frigidaire Gas Ranges Due to Potential Burn Hazard
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers should immediately stop using the affected ovens and reach out to Electrolux Group at 866-291-7633 from 8:30 am to 8 p.m. ET, Monday to Friday. They can also email gasovenburnerrecall@electrolux.com or visit www.GasOvenBurnerRecall.com and www.frigidaire.com for further details.

recalls.Description: Lifetime Brands has announced a recall for its BUILT LUUM collection of light up tumblers, which are available in 16 oz. and 18 oz. sizes. These tumblers, featuring festive designs for winter holidays and the Fourth of July, contain a bottom compartment with an LED light powered by a button cell battery. The product details, including the theme names and corresponding item numbers, can be found on the underside label or hangtag of the tumblers.
recalls.Title: Lifetime Brands Issues Recall for BUILT LUUM Light Up Tumblers Due to Choking and Battery Ingestion Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach out to Lifetime Brands at 888-561-2269 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or via email at Recall@Builtny.com. More information is available online at https://www.builtny.com/pages/recall or by visiting www.builtny.com and selecting 'Recall' at the bottom of the page.

Injuries.Name: Lifetime Brands has received one report of a child gaining access to the tumbler's button cell battery and putting it in the mouth. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available at Kroger, Hy-Vee, Meijer, Nordstrom Rack, and Winn-Dixie stores across the country, as well as online at Builtny.com from October 2024 through December 2025, priced between $10 and $15.

Importers.Name: Lifetime Brands, Inc., owner of the BUILT brand, of Garden City, New York

Hazards.Name: Risk of choking and ingestion due to accessible button cell batteries if tumblers break.

Remedies.Name: Immediately stop using the tumblers, keep them out of children's reach, and dispose of the battery properly. Contact Lifetime Brands for a full refund, which includes disposing of the LED component and uploading a photo of the product in the trash via the Product Recall Refund Form at https://www.builtny.com/pages/recall. Note: Dispose of button cell batteries following local hazardous waste guidelines.

recalls.metaDescription: Lifetime Brands recalls BUILT LUUM tumblers due to choking and battery hazards. Learn how to get a refund and safely dispose of the product.

recalls.Description: Curtis International has expanded its recall to include the Frigidaire-brand 6-can minifridge model EFMIS121, alongside previously recalled models EFMIS129, EFMIS137, EFMIS149, and EFMIS175. These minifridges, identifiable by the 'Frigidaire' logo on the front, may have certain serial numbers that pose fire and burn hazards due to potential short circuits in their electrical components. The affected serial numbers for model EFMIS121 range from A2001 to A2310.
recalls.Title: Curtis International Expands Recall of Frigidaire Minifridges Due to Fire and Burn Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For further information, consumers can contact Curtis International Ltd. toll-free at 888-727-0198 from 8 a.m. to 12 a.m. ET, Monday through Friday, email recall@curtiscs.com, or visit www.recallrtr.com/minifridge.

Products.Name: Frigidaire 6-can Minifridge Model EFMIS121
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 330,000, Curtis International previously recalled 634,000 minifridges in July 2024

Images.Caption: Recalled Curtis International Minifridge Model EFMIS121 – serial numbers between A2001 to A2310

Injuries.Name: Curtis International has received at least six reports of the model EFMIS121 minifridges catching fire, with property damages.

Manufacturers.Name: ShangYu North Electron Manufacture Co. Ltd., of China

Retailers.Name: Available exclusively at Target stores and online at Target.com from January 2020 to October 2023 for approximately $30

Importers.Name: Curtis International Ltd., of Canada

Hazards.Name: Fire and burn hazards due to short-circuiting electrical components

Remedies.Name: Stop using the minifridges, register for a refund, and dispose of the units properly

recalls.metaDescription: Curtis International recalls Frigidaire minifridges due to fire hazards. Learn how to get a refund and safely dispose of your unit.

recalls.Description: Sanven Technology is recalling Vevor ice crushing machines intended for home use due to a significant fire hazard. These ice crushers, marketed as Ice Shavers, Ice Crushers, and Snow Cone Makers, feature a handle that activates a cutter when pressed down to feed ice into a chute. The models affected are BY-300 and BY-300 FS, available in yellow, red, silver, and blue, with the brand name and model number labeled on the top.
recalls.Title: Sanven Technology Issues Recall for Vevor Ice Crushers Due to Fire Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers are advised to stop using the recalled ice crushers immediately and contact Sanven Technology for a full refund. Reach out via their toll-free number at 855-599-6320 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. PT, Monday through Friday, or email recalling@vevor.com. Additional information can be found online at https://www.vevor.com/pages/vevor-ice-shaver-recall or by visiting www.vevor.com and selecting 'Product Recall.'

Products.Name: Vevor Ice Crushers

recalls.Description: HydroJug is recalling its 14-ounce sport tumblers, which may pose a choking hazard due to a potential issue with the handle's rivets. These tumblers, identifiable by the serial number 235010 or lacking a serial number beneath the rubber boot, were available in five designs: Cowgirl, Daisy Checkers, Dinosaur, Sport, and Bows. Parents should be aware of this safety concern.
recalls.Title: HydroJug Recalls 14-Ounce Children's Sport Tumblers Due to Choking Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For more information, contact HydroJug via email at service@thehydrojug.com or visit https://www.thehydrojug.com/recall. You can also access details by clicking on the "Recall" link at the bottom of their homepage.

Images.Caption: Recalled 14 oz. Sport Tumbler in Cowgirl, Sports, Daisy Checkers, Dinosaur and Bows patterns
Recalled 14 oz. Sport Tumbler (with rubber boot removed)
Recalled 14 oz. Sport Tumblers bottom view (serial number 2350101 location shown on the left, no serial number shown on the right)

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 656 reports of the rivets on the handle coming loose. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available at Academy Sports, Scheels, Combined Sales Company, Gordons Ace Hardware, Bucks Ace, Kents Grocery, Basin Sports, and online at Hydrojug.com, Amazon.com, Well.ca, and iHerb.com from May 2025 through June 2025 for approximately $25.

Hazards.Name: Risk of choking due to loose rivets on the handle that may detach.

Remedies.Name: Consumers are advised to stop using the recalled tumbler immediately and reach out to HydroJug for a complimentary replacement lid. To initiate the process, email HydroJug at service@thehydrojug.com with your order details, shipping address, and photos showing the bottom of the tumbler and the lid marked "Recalled." HydroJug will verify your information and send a new lid.

recalls.metaDescription: HydroJug recalls 14-ounce children's sport tumblers due to choking hazard. Learn how to get a free replacement lid.

recalls.Description:
Tempo USA has announced a recall of all Ambiano electric pressure cookers identified by model numbers 93550 (KY-318B), 96775 (KY-318A), and 98527 (6L-PC). These cookers, available in six-quart and eight-quart sizes, feature the Ambiano brand name on the front and a model number label located on the bottom. Consumers should be aware of the serious burn hazard associated with these products. 
The danger arises when the pressure-cooker lid is opened prematurely, before enough steam pressure has been released. This can lead to hot contents escaping and potentially causing burn injuries to users.
recalls.Title: Tempo USA Issues Recall for Ambiano Electric Pressure Cookers Due to Burn Risks; Injuries Reported
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach ALDI at 800-325-7894 from 9 a.m. to 8 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, visit https://help.aldi.us/contact-form-product, email Tempo at serviceusa@tempo.org, or go to aldi.us and click on "Product Recalls" for further details.

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 11 reports of incidents, in which the contents were expelled under pressure, including eight reports of severe burn injuries.

Retailers.Name: Available at ALDI stores across the U.S. from January 2016 to December 2019 for approximately $40.

Importers.Name: Tempo International (USA) Inc., of Aventura, Florida

Hazards.Name: Risk of burn injuries due to premature lid opening

Remedies.Name: Stop using the recalled cookers immediately and contact Tempo or ALDI for a refund. Return the cooker to any ALDI store for a full refund or email Tempo with a photo of the unplugged cooker and model details.

recalls.Description: Sunbeam Products is recalling Oster French Door Countertop Ovens, specifically models TSSTTVFDXL, TSSTTVFDDG, TSSTTVFDMAF, and TSSTTVFDDAF. These ovens feature a glass door with metal handles that can unexpectedly close, creating a burn hazard for users. Model numbers can be found on a label located on the back of the oven, and the original packaging also displays this information. Each model offers different cooking options and control types, with 'Oster' engraved on the front.
recalls.Title: Sunbeam Products Issues Recall for Over One Million Oster French Door Countertop Ovens Due to Potential Burn Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Sunbeam Products Inc. at 800-334-0759 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or visit https://recall.oster.com or www.oster.com and click on 'Product Recalls' for further details.

Products.Name: Oster French Door Countertop Oven

recalls.Description:
Wuensche USA has announced a recall of all Ambiano Cotton Candy Makers, identified by model number 836098. These machines feature a red or teal base with a transparent plastic cover, and both the brand name and model number can be found on the packaging and the underside of the unit. Consumers should be aware of the potential fire hazard associated with these products. 
The issue arises when the heating element ignites sugar if the cotton candy maker is used without the designated sugar receptacle, creating a serious fire risk. To ensure safety, consumers are advised to stop using the product immediately.
recalls.Title: Wuensche USA Issues Recall for Ambiano Cotton Candy Makers Due to Fire Risk; Available at ALDI
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach out to ALDI at their toll-free number (800) 325-7894 from 9 a.m. to 8 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday. Additional support is available online at https://help.aldi.us/contact-form-product, or via email to Wuensche USA at service@801service.net. More information can also be found by visiting aldi.us and selecting 'Product Recalls' at the bottom of the page.

Images.Caption: Recalled Ambiano Cotton Candy Maker – Red
Recalled Ambiano Cotton Candy Maker – Teal
Recalled Ambiano Cotton Candy Maker – Rating Label

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 12 reports of sparking or fire. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available at ALDI stores nationwide from August 2024 to September 2024 for $15.

Importers.Name: Wuensche USA, Chicago, Illinois

Hazards.Name: Fire hazard due to potential ignition of sugar

Remedies.Name: Stop using the recalled cotton candy maker and contact Wuensche or ALDI for a refund. Return the intact unit to any ALDI store for a full refund or email Wuensche with a photo of the unplugged machine and model number.

recalls.metaDescription: Wuensche USA recalls Ambiano Cotton Candy Makers due to fire hazards. Contact ALDI for refunds and safety information.

recalls.Description: Drinkmate is recalling specific 1-liter Carbonation Bottles made from clear polyethylene terephthalate (PET) with colored plastic caps and bases in red, blue, white, and black. The affected bottles have expiration dates ranging from 01/2026 to 10/2026, clearly marked on the side. Only bottles that fall within these expiration dates are part of this recall. These bottles were sold individually and included in some Drinkmate OmniFizz starter kits.
recalls.Title: Drinkmate Issues Recall for 1-Liter Carbonation Bottles Due to Explosion Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Drinkmate at 844-812-6241 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, email Support@idrinkproducts.com, or visit https://idrinkproducts.com/pages/drinkmate-safety-recall-of-1l-pet-bottles for more details.

Products.Name: Drinkmate 1L Carbonation Bottles
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 106,200 (In addition, about 5,000 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Drinkmate 1L Carbonation Bottles
Recalled Drinkmate 1L Carbonation Bottle label with expiration date

Injuries.Name: Drinkmate has received eight reports of the bottle exploding while in use, including four reports resulting in lacerations, impact injuries and hearing damage.

Retailers.Name: Available at iDrinkproducts.com, Walmart.com, Amazon.com, Target.com, and HomeDepot.com from April 2023 through October 2024, priced at approximately $20 individually or $130 as part of some Drinkmate OmniFizz starter kits.

Importers.Name: IDrink Products Inc. dba Drinkmate, Inc., of Saline, Michigan

Hazards.Name: Explosion risk leading to serious impact, laceration, and hearing damage hazards.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease using the recalled bottles and contact Drinkmate for a complimentary replacement. A code will be provided for ordering the free replacement bottle, with shipping included.

recalls.metaDescription: Drinkmate recalls 1L Carbonation Bottles due to explosion risks. Stop using immediately and contact for a free replacement.

recalls.Description: Synergy Housewares has announced a recall of Wolfgang Puck petite tea kettles that feature a removable tea infuser basket. The product is identified by the name 'Wolfgang Puck Petite Kettle' along with a six-digit lot code located on the bottom of the kettle (022020, 022021, 022022, 042022, 022023, or 032023).
recalls.Title: Synergy Housewares Issues Recall for Wolfgang Puck Petite Tea Kettles Due to Burn Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Synergy Housewares at 855-837-4111, available from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or visit https://claims.synergyhousewares.com/recall for more information.

Images.Caption: Recalled Wolfgang Puck Petite Kettle
Wolfgang Puck Petite Kettle appears on the kettle's underside

Injuries.Name: Synergy Housewares has received at least 10 reports of incidents involving the kettle lids, including 5 resulting in injuries such as burns or scalding from the lid falling off the kettle during use.

Retailers.Name: Available on HSN.com and through the HSN television network from August 2020 to January 2025, priced around $20.

Importers.Name: Synergy Housewares LLC, of Coconut Creek, Florida

Hazards.Name: Loose infuser basket lid that can detach during use, creating a burn hazard.

recalls.Description: Leatherman has issued a recall for its Charge Plus and Charge Plus TTi multi-tools due to a potential laceration hazard. The Charge Plus models are available in black and stainless-steel finishes, and include a black MOLLE or nylon sheath. The Charge Plus TTi is offered in stainless-steel with a black nylon sheath. Each tool features the 'Leatherman' name etched on the body.
recalls.Title: Leatherman Charge Plus Multi-Tools Recalled Due to Laceration Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Leatherman at 800-847-8665 from 6 a.m. to 5 p.m. PT, Monday through Friday, email charge@leatherman.com, or visit www.leatherman.com/pages/charge-recall for more details.

Injuries.Name: None reported

Manufacturers.Name: Leatherman Tool Group Inc., of Portland, Oregon

Retailers.Name: Available at REI, various hardware and cutlery stores nationwide, and online at Leatherman.com, Amazon.com, and REI.com from July 2024 to March 2025, priced between $150 and $200.

Hazards.Name: Laceration hazard due to knife blade tip not fully retracting into handle

recalls.Description: Walmart is recalling the Ozark Trail 64 oz Stainless Steel Insulated Water Bottles, identified by model number 83-662. These silver bottles feature a black one-piece screwcap lid and measure 4.41 x 4.41 x 11.5 inches, with the Ozark Trail logo displayed on the side. The model number is not printed on the bottle itself. The recall is prompted by safety concerns regarding the lid's potential to eject forcefully, which could lead to serious impact and laceration injuries when opened after storing certain beverages over time.
